# Hash-Provenance Note — `rf-ev-001` fixture bundle

**Task**: P1-T3 (multi-bundle-conversion-e1, Phase 1 — Rights-Aware Vendoring & Fixtures)
**Source `rf` run**: `RF-EV-001` — `rf_run_20260717_rf_ev_001_pediatric_cds_backfill`
(intent `intent_research_20260717_rf_ev_001_pediatric_cds_backfill`; bundle id
`bundle_20260718_intent_research_20260717_rf_ev_001`; local mirror read from
`research-foundry/runs/rf_run_20260717_rf_ev_001_pediatric_cds_backfill/`; confirmed verified via
the run's own `reviews/verification.yaml` — `passed: true`, `exit_code: 0`,
`generated_at: '2026-07-18T17:09:50-04:00'` — and cross-checked against
`docs/project_plans/expansion/rf-handoff/RESULTS.md` §1, which records RF-EV-001 as `verified`,
6 source cards, 48 claims (35 supported / 8 inference / 5 speculation, 0 unsupported/mixed/
contradicted)).
**Selection rationale**: RF-EV-001 is one of this plan's 4 named clinical-bundle conversion
targets (FR-2) — never `REG-001`/`REG-004`, both legal-review-flagged and out of scope per FR-4/
R-7 — and generalizes this program's existing `RF-CBC-001` fixture pattern to a second bundle
(the anemia backfill run) rather than the neutropenia/CBC-establish run `rf-cbc-001` already
covers.
**Generated by**: `node scripts/evidence/generate-rf-fixture.mjs --run-dir <mirror-of-RF-EV-001>
--slug ev-001` (P1-T1's bundle-parametrized generator). Re-running the generator against the same
run directory reproduces this fixture tree byte-identically (`--check` exits 0); zero network
calls occur during generation.
**Derived**: 2026-07-22.

## 1. Content-rights disposition (applies to every passage in this bundle)

Every one of the 6 source cards in `RF-EV-001` carries the same `usage` block:

```
usage: {allowed_for_public_output: false, allowed_for_work_output: true,
        allowed_for_personal_meatywiki: true, citation_required: true,
        quote_limit_notes: "Short excerpts only."}
```

**No passage in this run is positively confirmed rights-clear for committing verbatim text to
this repository.** Per ADR-0002 / D-EP3-4's rights-restricted fallback (the same disposition
`rf-cbc-001` already applies, and this PRD's own §8 assumption that the 4 new bundles would match
`RF-CBC-001`'s restrictive disposition), this fixture therefore defaults **every single passage**
to the restricted disposition: the record retains an immutable **SHA-256 hash** of the original
verbatim excerpt plus its **precise selector** (the sibling `locator` field and the source card's
own `evidence_id`), but never the exact article text itself. This is a uniform disposition, not a
per-passage judgment call: 6/6 source cards → 35/35 extracted passages → **rights disposition:
restricted (hash + selector only)**, 0/35 positively confirmed rights-clear.

| # | Source card | Title | Publisher | Evidence points (passages) | Disposition |
|---|---|---|---|---:|---|
| 00 | `src_20260718_rfev001_00` | Prevention, Screening, Diagnosis, and Treatment of Iron Deficiency and Iron Deficiency Anemia in Infants, Children, and Adolescents: Clinical Report | American Academy of Pediatrics (Pediatrics) | 7 | restricted |
| 01 | `src_20260718_rfev001_01` | Guideline on haemoglobin cutoffs to define anaemia in individuals and populations | World Health Organization | 6 | restricted |
| 02 | `src_20260718_rfev001_02` | Anemia in the pediatric patient | American Society of Hematology (Blood) | 5 | restricted |
| 03 | `src_20260718_rfev001_03` | Recommended Actions Based on Blood Lead Level | US Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) | 5 | restricted |
| 04 | `src_20260718_rfev001_04` | Clinical Decision Support Software: Guidance for Industry and Food and Drug Administration Staff | US Food and Drug Administration (CDRH, Digital Health Center of Excellence) | 5 | restricted |
| 05 | `src_20260718_rfev001_05` | Laboratory diagnosis of G6PD deficiency. A British Society for Haematology Guideline | British Society for Haematology / British Journal of Haematology (John Wiley & Sons Ltd) | 7 | restricted |
| | | | **Total** | **35** | **35/35 restricted** |

Note: the run's claim ledger carries **48** claims total (35 supported, 8 inference, 5
speculation, 0 mixed/contradicted/unsupported per `evidence_bundle.yaml.counts` and
`rf-handoff/RESULTS.md` §1) — a larger count than the 35 extracted passages above because
several claims are synthesized (inference/speculation) from, or combine, more than one
extracted point rather than mapping one-to-one with a passage. Passage-level rights disposition
(this section) and claim-level synthesis status (the ledger) are two different axes of the same
bundle; neither overrides the other.

## 2. What was sanitized, and how

Only the **verbatim exact-passage excerpts** were redacted — everything else in the bundle
(claim ledger, extraction cards, report, verification record, RF's own paraphrased summaries,
population/assay/lifecycle metadata, source metadata, locators) is copied byte-for-byte
unmodified, because none of it reproduces the underlying articles' text at length. This was
verified mechanically, not only by convention: the generator (`assertNeverCarriesExcerptField`)
fails closed if any of these artifact kinds contains a `quote:`/`passage_locator:` construct, and
generation completed with exit code 0.

- `sources/src_*.md` — each source card's `extracted_points[].quote` field (the YAML frontmatter
  verbatim excerpt) and the matching `— quote: "..."` line in the markdown "Key evidence" section
  were replaced with
  `[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:<hash>]`,
  where `<hash>` is the SHA-256 of the original excerpt's exact bytes (both occurrences of the
  same excerpt hash identically, so a reviewer can confirm the YAML and markdown copies agree
  without ever seeing the text). The embedded excerpt inside any `threshold.passage_locator`
  flow-map value (which mixes a human-readable selector prefix with a second copy of the verbatim
  excerpt) was likewise replaced by its own SHA-256 hash — the human-readable selector is
  preserved undiminished in the sibling `locator` field one level up, so no addressability is
  lost. The generator's own post-redaction self-audit (`assertKnownExcerptsRedacted`) confirmed
  every known excerpt's placeholder is present in the redacted output before any file was
  written.
- `extractions/ext_*.yaml` — copied unmodified; mechanically confirmed (by the generator's
  `assertNeverCarriesExcerptField` guard, not only by human inspection) to carry no `quote`/
  verbatim-excerpt field across all 6 files.
- `claims/claim_ledger.yaml`, `reports/report_draft.md`, `reviews/verification.yaml`,
  `research_brief.md`, `swarm_plan.yaml`, `evidence_bundle.yaml`, `writebacks/ccdash_event.yaml`
  — copied unmodified; likewise mechanically confirmed to contain no verbatim source-article
  excerpts (claim text and report sentences are RF's own paraphrased/synthesized prose with
  `[claim:clm_id]` references, not reproduced article text).

Redaction counts (per source card, counted directly against the committed fixture bytes): 35
frontmatter `quote` fields, 33 mirrored markdown-body "Key evidence" bullet quotes (not every
point's markdown bullet restates its `quote:`; the frontmatter is the authoritative copy), and 17
embedded `threshold.passage_locator` excerpts — **85 total redactions** across the 6 source
cards. This generator run does not emit a separate machine-readable per-redaction ledger file (no
`passage-hash-ledger.json.txt` is committed for this fixture) — FR-3's required sections for this
note are the run `run_id`, the bundle SHA-256, the per-source-card rights-disposition table, and
the passage-count summary, all of which are given above and in §3 below; per-line ledger detail is
not part of this task's acceptance criteria.

## 3. Bundle hash (fixture as committed)

**Verified**: `node scripts/evidence/generate-rf-fixture.mjs --run-dir <mirror-of-RF-EV-001>
--slug ev-001 --check` exits 0 against the committed fixture (regenerating in memory and diffing
every file — no drift). `npm run check` passes with `modules/anemia/**` untouched.

SHA-256 computed over the sorted `relative_path:sha256(bytes)` pairs of every artifact file in
this fixture directory (i.e. the mirrored `RF-EV-001` bundle: `evidence_bundle.yaml`,
`research_brief.md`, `swarm_plan.yaml`, `claims/claim_ledger.yaml`, `reports/report_draft.md`,
`reviews/verification.yaml`, `writebacks/ccdash_event.yaml`, all 6 `sources/src_*.md`, all 6
`extractions/ext_*.yaml` — 19 files total; this note is excluded from the bundle hash as
fixture-derivation metadata, not `rf` bundle content):

```
bundleSha256: sha256:8b933a9e7e68644c8e43eeb2959152c9b0799c2a97981d0b65ab1187005ef530
fileCount: 19
```

## 4. Directory layout (mirrors `evidence_bundle.yaml.artifacts`)

```
tests/fixtures/rf-ev-001/
  evidence_bundle.yaml          (copied unmodified)
  research_brief.md             (copied unmodified)
  swarm_plan.yaml                (copied unmodified)
  claims/claim_ledger.yaml       (copied unmodified)
  reports/report_draft.md        (copied unmodified)
  reviews/verification.yaml      (copied unmodified)
  writebacks/ccdash_event.yaml    (copied unmodified)
  sources/src_20260718_rfev001_{00..05}.md   (sanitized — see §2)
  extractions/ext_20260718_*.yaml (6 files, copied unmodified)
  HASH-PROVENANCE.md             (this note)
```

Paths are resolved exactly as `evidence_bundle.yaml.artifacts` declares them
(`source_cards_dir: sources/`, `extraction_cards_dir: extractions/`, etc.) so that the
`rf-bundle-to-kb-pack` converter's `inspect`/`verify`/`propose` verbs can resolve every artifact
without any hard-coded assumption, exactly as they already do for `tests/fixtures/rf-cbc-001/`.

## 5. Non-goals of this note

This note does not reopen this plan's decisions block or its OQ-1..OQ-5. It documents only how
this one fixture bundle was derived from `RF-EV-001` and what was redacted and why — a rights/
hash record, not a clinical-content or rule-authoring decision. Nothing in `tests/fixtures/
rf-ev-001/` is production clinical evidence: it is an unconverted `rf` bundle mirror, staged for
the `rf-bundle-to-kb-pack` converter's later `propose` step (EF-WP1, not yet run against this
fixture).
